mnemonics: TRY AGAIN, TRY WITHIN: Outer try handles the big picture, inner try handles specific details.
keyPoints: An outer try block can contain one or more inner try blocks, allowing for layered exception handling.
If an inner try block does not have a catch handler for a particular exception, the search propagates to the outer try block's catch handlers.
Nested try blocks are useful for isolating error-prone code segments within a larger operation that also needs its own error handling.
commonMistakes: Overcomplicating code with unnecessary nesting; use it only when a specific inner code section requires distinct handling.
Forgetting that an uncaught exception in an inner block will propagate to the outer block, potentially being caught by the wrong handler.
Incorrectly ordering catch blocks (more specific exceptions must be caught before more general ones) within each level of the try-catch structure.
